passages.text: The Industrial Revolution was a period of profound transformation. It (8) ---- the transition to new manufacturing processes in Europe and the United States. This era (9) ---- a shift from hand production methods to machines. (10) ---- the economic growth was unprecedented, the social impact was also significant. Many workers (11) ---- harsh conditions in urban centers. Therefore, it is essential to (12) ---- the multifaceted nature of this historical epoch.

The discovery of exoplanets has revolutionized our understanding of the universe. For decades, the existence of planets orbiting stars other than our Sun was a matter of speculation. However, with the advent of advanced telescopes like Kepler, thousands of such planets have been identified. Scientists are particularly interested in the 'habitable zone,' the region around a star where conditions might allow for liquid water. While finding a 'Second Earth' remains elusive, the diversity of planetary systems discovered so far suggests that our solar system may not be as typical as once believed.

Cognitive dissonance is a psychological phenomenon that occurs when an individual holds two or more contradictory beliefs or values. This inconsistency leads to feelings of discomfort, which motivates the person to resolve the conflict. People often justify their behavior or change their attitudes to regain mental equilibrium. For instance, a smoker who knows that smoking is harmful might convince themselves that the health risks are exaggerated. Understanding cognitive dissonance helps explain why people often resist new information that challenges their existing worldview.

options.A: Sosyal medyanın yükselişi, insanların iletişim kurma ve bilgi paylaşma biçimini kökten değiştirdi.

questions.question: It is likely that the drought will lead to a decrease in agricultural production this year.

options.B: The drought is expected to cause a drop in crop yields this year.
options.C: There might be a drought if agricultural production decreases.
options.D: This year's drought was caused by a lack of agricultural planning.
options.E: A decrease in agricultural production is necessary to avoid drought.

questions.question: No sooner had the meeting started than the fire alarm went off.

options.A: The meeting started right after the fire alarm rang.
options.B: The fire alarm rang just as the meeting was beginning.
options.C: The meeting was interrupted by a fire alarm before it could start.
options.D: As soon as the fire alarm stopped, the meeting began.
options.E: The fire alarm went off because the meeting had started.

options.D: No one was surprised when the startup became a global giant.

questions.question:
Jane: I've been reading about the benefits of a plant-based diet. 
Mark: It seems to be gaining a lot of popularity lately. 
Jane: ----

options.A: I think it's just a passing fad.
options.B: Yes, but many people still prefer eating meat.
options.C: True, and many people are also concerned about the environmental impact of meat production.
options.D: I'm not sure if I could ever give up cheese.
options.E: What are some of the health benefits you mentioned?

questions.question:
Professor: Your thesis proposal on renewable energy is quite ambitious.

Professor: ----

options.A: It's perfect as it is; don't change anything.
options.B: However, you might want to narrow down your scope to a specific region.
options.C: Why did you choose this topic in the first place?
options.D: I don't think you have enough data for this study.
options.E: The deadline for submission is next week.

questions.question: Renewable energy is becoming increasingly important as the world seeks to reduce its reliance on fossil fuels. Solar, wind, and hydroelectric power offer cleaner alternatives that produce fewer greenhouse gas emissions. ----. Governments around the world are investing heavily in these technologies to meet climate goals.

options.A: Fossil fuels are still the most reliable source of energy.
options.B: Furthermore, the cost of renewable energy has dropped significantly in recent years.
options.C: Nuclear power remains a controversial topic in many countries.
options.D: The transition to green energy will take several decades.
options.E: Energy consumption is expected to double by 2050.

questions.question: The concept of emotional intelligence has gained widespread recognition in the workplace. It involves the ability to perceive, understand, and manage one's own emotions as well as those of others. ----. Research shows that employees with high emotional intelligence are more likely to be successful and effective leaders.

options.A: IQ is still considered the best predictor of job performance.
options.B: Many companies are now offering training programs to develop these skills.
options.C: Emotional intelligence is often confused with being overly sensitive.
options.D: Technical skills are more important than soft skills in many industries.
options.E: The term was first coined by psychologists in the early 1990s.

questions.question: Urbanization is a global trend that is transforming societies. As more people move to cities in search of better opportunities, urban areas are expanding at an unprecedented rate. ----. Issues such as housing shortages, traffic congestion, and pollution must be addressed to ensure sustainable growth.

options.B: This rapid growth presents both opportunities and challenges for city planners.
options.C: Cities are becoming more multicultural and diverse.
options.D: The quality of life in urban centers is generally higher than in rural areas.
options.E: Public transportation is the backbone of any modern city.

questions.question: Artificial intelligence is no longer just a concept of science fiction; it is now an integral part of our daily lives. From virtual assistants to personalized recommendations on streaming platforms, AI technology is everywhere. ----. As AI continues to evolve, its impact on society will only grow stronger.

options.C: It is also being used in healthcare to improve diagnosis and treatment.
options.D: AI will eventually replace most human jobs.
options.E: Computers are becoming smarter every year.

questions.question: Hardly any students passed the exam due to its unexpected difficulty.

options.C: A few students managed to pass the exam despite the difficulty.
options.D: The exam was difficult, but the students were well-prepared.
options.E: No one was able to pass the exam because it was too difficult.

options.D: IV

options.C: It may not be as typical as previously thought.
options.D: It is the center of the universe.

options.D: They only orbit stars that are similar to our Sun.

questions.question: What causes the feeling of discomfort in cognitive dissonance?

options.A: A lack of information about a particular subject.
options.B: Holding contradictory beliefs or values at the same time.
options.C: Being challenged by someone with a different worldview.
options.D: The inability to change one's behavior in a difficult situation.
options.E: A lack of mental equilibrium due to stress.

questions.question: How might a smoker resolve cognitive dissonance according to the passage?

options.A: By quitting smoking immediately.
options.B: By ignoring all information about health risks.
options.C: By convincing themselves that the risks are not that high.
options.D: By seeking professional help for their addiction.
options.E: By changing their lifestyle completely.

questions.question: What is one of the broader implications of understanding cognitive dissonance?

options.A: It helps in developing new psychological treatments.
options.B: It explains why people are often resistant to new and challenging information.
options.C: It shows that people are always rational in their decision-making.
options.D: It proves that human behavior is entirely predictable.
options.E: It encourages people to hold more contradictory beliefs.
